title: Sudden breathlessness post-op

chart.oneLiner: 58F presents with sudden dyspnea and right-sided pleuritic chest pain, 5 days after a total hip replacement.

opening: All of a sudden I couldn't catch my breath, and it hurts when I breathe in.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 58-year-old woman, anxious, tachypneic. Recovering from recent surgery, limited mobility. Cooperative.

HPI: Abrupt onset of dyspnea and right-sided pleuritic chest pain this afternoon. Five days post total hip replacement, mostly bedbound since. Mild right calf discomfort for a day (only if asked / examined). NO fever, NO purulent cough, NO orthopnea, NO prior cardiac history.
PMH: osteoarthritis (s/p right THR 5 days ago), obesity. MEDS: acetaminophen, oxycodone PRN; was supposed to get DVT prophylaxis (only if asked). SOCIAL: nonsmoker.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: anxious, tachypneic, mild distress.
- Vitals: BP 108/68, HR 116, RR 26, 37.3°C, SpO2 89% RA.
- Lungs: clear bilaterally, no focal crackles or consolidation.
- Cardiac: tachycardic, regular; loud P2 if specifically auscultated.
- Extremities: RIGHT calf mildly swollen and tender, surgical dressing intact on right hip; left leg normal.
- Neck: JVP mildly elevated if examined.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the calf symptoms, the missed prophylaxis, or the immobility unless asked about leg symptoms, blood thinners/prophylaxis, or activity/mobility.
- The calf findings and loud P2 are revealed only if examined.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.ABG: pH 7.49, PaCO2 29, PaO2 60 — hypoxemia with respiratory alkalosis.
labs.CTPE: CT pulmonary angiogram: bilateral segmental pulmonary emboli; RV/LV ratio borderline.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possible; no acute ST elevation.
ekg.verify: From the LITFL 'ECG Changes in Pulmonary Embolism' library page — download an example tracing that matches: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possib … and self-host with credit to litfl.com.

ekg.imageDescription: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possible; no acute ST elevation.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray (PA)
cxr.findings: Essentially clear — no consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax (a classic 'clear film with marked hypoxemia' clue).
cxr.verify: Pick a CC normal/near-normal PA chest film (the point is a clear film despite hypoxemia).
cxr.imageDescription: Essentially clear — no consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax (a classic 'clear film with marked hypoxemia' clue).
searchTerms: chest radiograph Essentially clear consolidation effusion

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential diagnosis (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Pulmonary embolism (leading & CANT-MISS,9; accepts PE). Acute coronary syndrome (CANT-MISS,4). Pneumonia/atelectasis (core,3). Heart failure (core,2). Post-op atelectasis (bonus,2). Names PE with risk factors — recent surgery, immobility (core,5).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Pulmonary embolism. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Acute coronary syndrome. Also reasonable: Pneumonia/atelectasis; Heart failure.

coreActions.item: Names PE with risk factors — recent surgery, immobility

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial diagnostic workup do you want to order? (Consider pretest probability.)
steps.rubric: CT pulmonary angiogram (CANT-MISS,6; accepts CTPE/CTA). EKG (core,3). CXR (core,3). Troponin (core,2). Recognizes high pretest probability — go to imaging; D-dimer unhelpful post-op (bonus,3). PENALTY: relying on a D-dimer to exclude PE in a high-probability post-op patient (-3).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: EKG; CXR; CBC; BMP; Troponin; D-dimer; ABG; CTPE.

bonusActions.item: Recognizes high pretest probability — go to imaging; D-dimer unhelpful post-op

penalties.item: relying on a D-dimer to exclude PE in a high-probability post-op patient

contraindicatedTests: relying on a D-dimer to exclude PE in a high-probability post-op patient

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = sinus tachycardia; possible S1Q3T3 / right heart strain; no acute STEMI. CREDIT: rate & rhythm (sinus tachy) (6); recognizes right-heart-strain pattern or S1Q3T3 if present (5); no acute ST-elevation MI (2); systematic read (2). PENALTY: calling a STEMI (-3).
keyFindings: recognizes right-heart-strain pattern or S1Q3T3 if present
criticalFindings: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possible; no acute ST elevation.
steps.idealAnswer: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possible; no acute ST elevation.

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Sinus tachycardia; S1Q3T3 pattern possible; right heart strain (T inversions V1–V3) possible; no acute ST elevation.

coreActions.item: recognizes right-heart-strain pattern or S1Q3T3 if present

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = essentially clear film. CREDIT: correctly identifies no consolidation/effusion/pneumothorax (6); notes the clinically important mismatch — clear film with significant hypoxemia points toward PE (4). PENALTY: inventing an infiltrate (-2).
keyFindings: notes the clinically important mismatch — clear film with significant hypoxemia points toward PE
criticalFindings: Essentially clear — no consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax (a classic 'clear film with marked hypoxemia' clue).
steps.idealAnswer: Essentially clear — no consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax (a classic 'clear film with marked hypoxemia' clue).

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Essentially clear — no consolidation, effusion, or pneumothorax (a classic 'clear film with marked hypoxemia' clue).

coreActions.item: notes the clinically important mismatch — clear film with significant hypoxemia points toward PE

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Given the CTPE result, what is your diagnosis and how do you risk-stratify?
steps.rubric: Acute pulmonary embolism (5). Assesses hemodynamic stability / RV strain to classify (low-risk vs submassive vs massive) (3). Links mild troponin + tachycardia to RV strain (bonus,2).

bonusActions.item: Links mild troponin + tachycardia to RV strain

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH VTE Management: anticoagulation, DOAC preferred (CANT-MISS,6); risk-stratify low/intermediate/high (4); thrombolysis (tPA) for high-risk/unstable (5); PERT consult (3); O2/supportive care, cautious IVF (3); IVC filter only if AC contraindicated (2); admit/monitor (2). PENALTY: IVC filter instead of AC when AC not contraindicated (-3); lytics in stable low-risk PE (-3).
steps.idealAnswer: Risk-stratify the PE. Anticoagulate unless contraindicated – DOAC preferred. Systemic thrombolysis (tPA) and PERT/SHOCK activation for high-risk (hemodynamically unstable) PE. Supplemental O2 (HFNC preferred); cautious IVF; norepinephrine for shock. IVC filter only if anticoagulation contraindicated.

bonusActions.item: IVC filter only if AC contraindicated

optionalActions: IVC filter only if AC contraindicated

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 58F presents with sudden dyspnea and right-sided pleuritic chest pain, 5 days after a total hip replacement. The diagnosis is Pulmonary embolism, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Neck: JVP mildly elevated if examined.
Lungs: clear bilaterally, no focal crackles or consolidation.
Lungs: clear bilaterally, no focal crackles or consolidation.
Extremities: RIGHT calf mildly swollen and tender, surgical dressing intact on right hip; left leg normal.
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Pulmonary embolism while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Pulmonary embolism and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.

Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.
